search_keywords.title: 🔑 Positions / Keywords for Search (one per line)
search_keywords.description:
Enter job titles or keywords to search on Indeed, one per line. The scraper builds search URLs automatically from these.

Example:
Python Developer
Backend Engineer
Django Developer

Leave blank if you are providing your own 'Indeed Search URLs' below.

search_country.title: 🌍 Country for Search
search_country.description: The country to search on Indeed. Determines which Indeed domain is used (e.g. indeed.com, uk.indeed.com).

search_location.title: 📍 Location for Search (optional)
search_location.description:
City, state, or region to search within. Leave blank to search nationwide or for remote jobs.

Examples:  San Francisco, CA  |  New York  |  Remote  |  London

date_filter.title: 📅 Date Posted Filter
date_filter.description: Only return jobs posted within this time window. Applied to keyword-based searches only — has no effect if you supply your own 'Indeed Search URLs'.

start_urls.title: 🔗 Indeed Search URLs (optional — overrides keyword search)
start_urls.description:
Advanced option: paste your own Indeed search URLs here. These take priority over the keyword/location/country fields above.

When to use this: if you want to use Indeed's filters (date posted, salary, job type) that aren't available through the keyword builder — just set up your search on Indeed with those filters and copy the URL.

Leave blank to use the keyword search fields above.

about_me.title: 🎯 Target Job Titles for AI Matching (one per line, optional)
about_me.description:
Enter the job titles you are targeting, one per line. The AI (JobBERT) scores each scraped job title against yours — only jobs meeting your minimum score are saved.

Leave blank to disable AI filtering and collect all jobs.

min_match_percentage.title: 🤖 Minimum AI Match Score (0–100)
min_match_percentage.description:
Only used when 'Target Job Titles' is filled in. Jobs scoring below this threshold are skipped.

Lower = more results, less targeted. Higher = fewer but more relevant.
Recommended: 40–90.

max_jobs.title: 📦 Total Jobs to Collect
max_jobs.description: The scraper stops once it has saved this many jobs total (across all keywords/URLs). Recommended: 50–500.

per_company_jobs.title: 🏢 Max Jobs per Company
per_company_jobs.description: Limits how many jobs from the same company are saved. Keeps results diverse.

scrape_company_details.title: 🏭 Scrape Company Details
scrape_company_details.description:
When enabled, the scraper visits each company's Indeed profile page to collect extra data: company size, industry, and a short description.

Note: this roughly doubles the number of page loads, so it will be slower and use more proxy bandwidth.

save_unique_only.title: ✅ Save Only Unique Job Listings
save_unique_only.description: When enabled, if the same job title at the same company appears more than once (e.g. across different search URLs), only the first occurrence is saved. Recommended to keep on.

skip_expired_jobs.title: ⏰ Skip Expired Jobs
skip_expired_jobs.description:
When enabled, jobs marked as expired on Indeed are silently skipped and not saved to your results.

Disable if you want to collect expired listings (e.g. for market research).

skip_ignore_related_jobs.title: 🚫 Skip Jobs Matching 'Keywords to Skip'
skip_ignore_related_jobs.description:
When enabled, any job whose description contains a word from your 'Keywords to Skip' list is silently skipped and not saved.

Disable if you want those jobs saved but flagged instead (the ignore_related field will be set to 'True' in the output).

follow_apply_redirect.title: 🔗 Follow Redirects for Apply Link
follow_apply_redirect.description:
When enabled, the scraper resolves the external 'Apply on company site' link to its final destination URL (following all redirects). Useful when you want the real company careers page URL instead of Indeed's redirect.

Slightly slower — only enable if you need resolved URLs.

ignore_companies.title: 🚫 Companies to Skip (optional)
ignore_companies.description:
Jobs from these companies will be ignored. One name per line.

Example:
Google
Amazon
Staffing Solutions Inc

ignore_related.title: 🚫 Keywords to Skip (optional)
ignore_related.description:
If a job description contains any of these words, that job is skipped (or flagged, depending on the 'Skip Jobs Matching Keywords' toggle). One keyword per line.

Example:
clearance required
C++
management consulting

processed_job_urls.title: ⏭️ Already-Processed Job URLs (skip duplicates across runs)
processed_job_urls.description:
Paste Indeed job URLs you already scraped in a previous run. The scraper extracts job IDs from these and skips them — so you never collect the same job twice across multiple runs.

Paste job URLs in the format:
https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=abc123def456

account_cookies.title: 🍪 Indeed Account Cookies (optional — recommended)
account_cookies.description:
Paste your Indeed session cookies as a JSON array. Lets the scraper act as a logged-in user for better results.

How to export cookies:
1. Log into Indeed in Chrome.
2. Install the 'Cookie-Editor' extension: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/nmckokihipjgplolmcmjakknndddifde
3. Click Export → Export as JSON and paste here.

proxy_list.title: 🌐 Proxy List (optional)
proxy_list.description:
Your own proxies, one per line. Rotated each time a browser context is recreated (every 1000 requests).

Supported formats:
  http://user:pass@host:port
  host:port:user:pass
  host:port

google_sheet_url.title: 📊 Google Sheet URL (optional)
google_sheet_url.description:
Export results to Google Sheets — no credentials needed.

Setup (one-time):
1. Open your Google Sheet.
2. Share → General access → 'Anyone with the link' → Editor.
3. Paste the URL here.

sheet_name.title: 📋 Google Sheet Tab Name
sheet_name.description: The worksheet tab where results are written. Created automatically if it doesn't exist.

concurrency.title: ⚡ Concurrency (advanced)
concurrency.description:
Number of parallel browser workers per phase (listing + processing). Total browser contexts = concurrency × 2.

Memory guide:
  1 GB RAM  → use 2
  2 GB RAM  → use 4
  4 GB RAM  → use 8

Higher = faster but uses more memory. OOM crashes if set too high for your memory allocation.
